description_long: This object is a rectangular metal radiator with a series of evenly spaced horizontal ridges running across its main face, giving it a corrugated appearance. The radiator is framed by two vertical side panels that are slightly angled outward, providing structural support and a modern industrial look. The surface is finished in a muted, metallic gray with subtle weathering and discoloration, suggesting exposure to urban environments. The side views reveal a thin profile with a blue-tinted strip running vertically, possibly for aesthetic or functional purposes such as heat dissipation or protection. There are no visible valves, knobs, or pipe connections on the exterior, indicating a minimalist design. The overall construction appears robust, suitable for installation in public or semi-public urban spaces such as garages, sidewalks, or building exteriors.
description: A rectangular, corrugated metal radiator with angled side panels and a weathered gray finish, featuring blue-tinted vertical strips on the sides.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full corrugated metal face and angled side panels.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and a blue-tinted vertical strip along the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the corrugated metal surface and angled sides.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and blue-tinted vertical strip.
